title: Jainism: A Guide for the Perplexed
excerpt: Sherry Fohr's accessible guide to Jainism, designed for students and general readers who want a clear, structured introduction to Jain beliefs, practices, and history.
keyIdeas: Jainism offers a coherent spiritual path centered on non-violence, non-attachment, and non-absolutism
The Jain tradition encompasses both rigorous asceticism for monks and a practical path for laypeople
Understanding Jainism requires seeing it as a living tradition, not just a set of ancient doctrines
keyTakeaways: Ahimsa (non-violence) is the foundation of all Jain practice and ethics
The Jain path distinguishes sharply between monastic and lay practice
Jain cosmology describes an eternal universe of endless cycles of time
Liberation requires destroying all karmic matter through ascetic practice
Jainism is a minority tradition in India but has global influence through its ethical teachings
whoShouldRead: Students needing a clear, structured introduction to Jainism
General readers curious about one of the world's oldest religions
Practitioners of other traditions wanting to understand Jain perspectives on non-violence
Anyone looking for a more accessible alternative to Dundas's scholarly treatment
whoShouldSkip: Advanced scholars needing detailed textual analysis
Those who prefer primary sources to secondary introductions
Readers seeking a specifically Jain devotional or insider perspective
